counterexample.note: rank=1, crossing=0. T maps every block to itself but non-uniformly.

TEST_002.root_cause: Crossing number counts BOUNDARY CROSSINGS (source-block != target-block). It misses intra-block non-uniformity, which also creates defect.
TEST_002.replacement_bound: rank(D_Pi) <= |{blocks B : T is not block-uniform on B}|
TEST_002.status: CANDIDATE THEOREM — strongly supported, not yet proved

TEST_003.hypothesis: Sharp rank phase transition exists as eps: structured -> random
TEST_003.finding: Rank saturates at min(k,n-k)=2 at eps=0.01. No sharp transition. Internal geometry (entropy, stable_rank) transitions smoothly. Entropy peaks near eps=0.5 then decreases (random matrix has structured singular spectrum).
TEST_003.open: Phase transition may appear with larger k (more blocks) or larger n. Needs TEST_003 at n=64, k=8.

TEST_008.algorithm: Start from initial partition. Repeatedly split each block by T-image-block membership. Stop when no splits occur.
TEST_008.key_finding: Algorithm achieves D=0 when refinement is possible. When T creates block merges (constant map), pure refinement cannot reach D=0. Starting from 1-block and refining recovers Nerode only with appropriate output observation.
TEST_008.candidate_theorem: Defect-guided refinement from output-induced initial partition converges to Nerode quotient in <= depth(T) steps.
TEST_008.hopcroft_comparison: Our algorithm is partition refinement without intelligent splitter ordering. Equivalent in correctness, O(n^3) vs Hopcroft O(n log n).

new_candidates: CAND-D2: Defect-guided refinement terminates in O(depth(T)) steps  [SUPPORTED]
CAND-D3: rank(D) saturates at min(k,n-k) for any eps>0 interpolation  [OBSERVED n=16]

quantum_channel_status.note: Document 9 quantum framework: correct quantum info primitives but trivial test cases (P=I). QV005 expected False but is True (basis states ARE DFS for dephasing). Substantive quantum tests require non-trivial subspace choices. No proven connection to classical AQARION/Kaprekar work yet.
quantum_channel_status.action: Defer quantum extension until OPEN-008 (D_Pi=0 characterization) is proved for classical FDDS.
